compatibility.title: AVIF Support Across Platforms
compatibility.description: AVIF is a newer format, so not all platforms support it yet. Here's where you might need to convert AVIF to GIF.

items.detail: Requires the free 'AV1 Video Extension' from Microsoft Store to view AVIF natively. Without it, convert to GIF for instant viewing.
macOS Ventura (13.0+) and later support AVIF in Preview and Safari. Older Mac versions need conversion to GIF or other formats.
iOS 16+ supports AVIF in Safari and Photos. For sharing with users on older iOS versions, convert to GIF for guaranteed compatibility.
Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Edge 121+, and Safari 16.4+ support AVIF. For broader reach including older browsers, GIF works everywhere.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Will converting AVIF to GIF reduce image quality?
faq.a1: GIF is limited to 256 colors, so photographic images will lose some color detail. For simple graphics, icons, and illustrations, the quality difference is minimal. For photos where quality matters most, consider JPG or PNG instead.
faq.a2: GIF supports binary transparency — pixels are either fully transparent or fully opaque. It does not support semi-transparency or smooth alpha gradients. For images with smooth transparent edges, convert to PNG or WebP instead.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert private photos here?
faq.a3: Yes. Avifkit converts images entirely in your browser using client-side technology. Your files are never uploaded to any server — they stay on your device throughout the entire conversion process.
faq.a4: AVIF is far superior in compression, color depth, and quality. However, GIF has universal compatibility — every browser, email client, and messaging app supports it. Convert to GIF when you need guaranteed display across all platforms.
faq.q5: How do I open AVIF files on Windows?
faq.a5: Windows 10 and 11 can display AVIF after installing the free 'AV1 Video Extension' from the Microsoft Store. Alternatively, convert your AVIF images to GIF here for instant viewing without installing anything.
faq.q6: Can I open AVIF on iPhone or Mac?
faq.a6: iOS 16+ and macOS Ventura (13.0+) support AVIF natively. For older Apple devices, convert to GIF for universal compatibility across all iOS and macOS versions.
faq.q7: Why are GIF files sometimes larger than AVIF?
faq.a7: GIF uses basic LZW compression from the 1980s, while AVIF uses modern AV1 compression. For complex images with many colors, GIF files can be significantly larger. GIF works best for simple graphics with few colors.
faq.q8: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a8: Each file can be up to 10 MB. Since processing happens entirely in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device's performance.
faq.q9: Can I batch convert multiple AVIF files to GIF?
faq.a9: Yes! Upload up to 30 AVIF files at once. Click Convert All, then download each file individually or as a ZIP archive.
faq.a10: AVIF (AV1 Image File Format) is a modern image format based on the AV1 video codec. It offers excellent compression and quality but isn't supported everywhere yet. Converting to GIF ensures your images can be viewed on any device or platform.
faq.q11: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a11: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Just open the page and start converting.
faq.q12: How fast is the AVIF to GIF conversion?
faq.a12: Conversion is nearly instant since it happens directly in your browser — there's no upload or download wait. Speed depends on your device, but most images convert in under a second.
